summary: I am currently here in Syracuse, New York, where I have settled after coming from the refugee camp. My family and I moved here around 2009 after my mother applied for us to come to America. Living in Syracuse has been a big change from the camp life I knew in Kenya, and it’s where I am now building my life with new opportunities.

I spent much of my childhood in a refugee camp in Kenya, where my mother and family lived after fleeing Somalia. My mother arrived in the camp around 1991 or 1992 when she was about 13 years old, and we stayed there until 2009. The camp was mostly populated by Somali people like us, and life there was simple but limited, with no real streets or schools for me to attend regularly.

My family originally came from Somalia, but due to conflict, we had to flee and ended up living in a refugee camp in Kenya. My mother escaped Somalia with friends and relatives, and many of those who fled with her still live in the camp. Somalia is where our roots are, but the instability there forced us to seek safety elsewhere.

My mother started the process to bring us to the United States around 2006, and after interviews and waiting, we finally came here in 2009. The U.S. represents new opportunities for me and my siblings, and my father wanted all his children to have the same chance to succeed here. Coming to America was a big transition from camp life, and it has opened doors that I never had before.
